questions.prompt: You fulfill orders by reading the Checkout Session on the success page. Users sometimes close the tab before redirect. Right fix?

choices.text: Fulfill via `checkout.session.completed` webhook, not the success URL redirect. Webhooks fire regardless of whether the user returned to your site. Treat webhooks as source of truth; success page is for UX only.

questions.explanation: Webhooks are durable; success URL redirects aren't. Build fulfillment on webhooks; success page is the UX courtesy for returning users.

questions.prompt: A developer built a custom card input form sending card numbers to your backend. Compliance audit fails. Why?

choices.text: Receiving raw card data triggers PCI-DSS scope — heavy compliance burden. Migrate to Stripe Elements: card data goes directly from browser to Stripe; your server never sees raw card numbers. Use Appearance API for custom design while keeping PCI scope minimal.

questions.explanation: Elements keep PCI scope minimal by handling card data client-side. Appearance API lets you match brand design without taking on full PCI compliance.

questions.prompt: A B2B SaaS charges $5K-$50K per invoice. Card fees are ~$150 per charge. Cost-saving move?

choices.text: Add ACH / bank debits for large invoices. ACH fees cap at ~$5 vs. percentage-based card fees. Settlement is slower (3-5 days) and chargeback windows different, but acceptable for B2B contracts. Often saves tens of thousands per year.

questions.explanation: ACH/SEPA fees are capped, not percentage-based. For large amounts, dramatically cheaper than cards. Trade-off: slower settlement and different chargeback rules; usually acceptable for B2B.

questions.prompt: What's the simplest way to enable the right payment methods per customer?

choices.text: Use `automatic_payment_methods: { enabled: true }` and configure supported methods in Stripe Dashboard. Stripe optimizes the subset shown per customer based on geo, currency, amount. One configuration; correct behavior automatically.

questions.explanation: Automatic payment methods is the canonical pattern. Configure once; Stripe optimizes per transaction.

questions.prompt: Subscription renewal off-session fails with `requires_action`. Right pattern?

choices.text: Catch `invoice.payment_action_required` webhook; mark user 'needs payment action'; surface banner + email. On next visit, complete saved PaymentIntent via Stripe.js (which handles 3DS). After auth, subscription resumes. Off-session 3DS requires user-present completion.

questions.explanation: Off-session 3DS needs user-present auth. Recovery flow: webhook → mark user → banner/email → user authenticates via Stripe.js → subscription resumes.
